# CWE-347 safe: Improper Verification of Cryptographic Signature (mitigated, pure stdlib)
lang: go
file: CWE-347-safe.pure.go
variant: pure-go
---
package sample

import (
	"crypto"
	"crypto/rsa"
	"crypto/sha256"
	"crypto/x509"
	"database/sql"
	"encoding/base64"
	"encoding/json"
	"encoding/pem"
	"errors"
	"net/http"
	"os"
	"strings"
)

func ApplyWebhookClaims(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, db *sql.DB) error {
	raw := r.Header.Get("X-Webhook-JWT")
	parts := strings.Split(raw, ".")
	if len(parts) != 3 {
		return errors.New("malformed token")
	}
	sig, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[2])
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	block, _ := pem.Decode([]byte(os.Getenv("WEBHOOK_JWT_PUB")))
	pub, err := x509.ParsePKIXPublicKey(block.Bytes)
	if err != nil {
		return err
	}
	rsaPub := pub.(*rsa.PublicKey)
	signed := parts[0] + "." + parts[1]
	sum := sha256.Sum256([]byte(signed))
	if rsa.VerifyPKCS1v15(rsaPub, crypto.SHA256, sum[:], sig) != nil {
		return errors.New("invalid signature")
	}
	payload, _ := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
	var claims map[string]interface{}
	_ = json.Unmarshal(payload, &claims)
	_, err = db.Exec("UPDATE operators SET role = ? WHERE id = ?", claims["role"], claims["sub"])
	if err != nil {
		http.Error(w, err.Error(), http.StatusInternalServerError)
		return err
	}
	w.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)
	return nil
}
